253.reason: [When encountering a single '|', the code correctly reports error 'a', but fails to skip the character, causing it to be reprocessed in the next iteration and potentially leading to infinite loop or duplicate error. Similarly for single '&'. The character should be consumed (not pushed back) when reporting the error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|', the code correctly reports error 'a', but fails to skip the character, causing it to be reprocessed in the next iteration and potentially leading to infinite loop or duplicate error. Similarly for single '&'. The character should be consumed (not pushed back) when reporting the error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|' or '&', the code correctly detects error 'a', but it does not skip the invalid character. As a result, the same character will be reprocessed in the next loop iteration, potentially causing an infinite loop or duplicate error reporting. The invalid character should be consumed (i.e., not decrementing pos) so that lexical analysis can continue.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|' or '&', the code correctly detects error 'a', but it does not skip the invalid character. As a result, the lexer may reprocess the same character in the next iteration, potentially causing an infinite loop or duplicate error reporting. However, more critically, after reporting the error, the code still falls through to add a token if `type != null`, but in this branch `type` remains null, so no token is added — which is correct. The real issue is that the error is reported, but the lexer continues processing without advancing past the erroneous single '&' or '|', and since `pos` was decremented, the same character will be read again in the next loop iteration, leading to an infinite loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [After reporting error 'a' for a single '&' or '|', the lexer does not skip the invalid character. The main loop will reprocess the same character in the next iteration because 'type' remains null and no token is emitted, potentially causing an infinite loop or duplicate error reports.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: if pos is at the last character of rawCode, pos++ leads to reading beyond the string bounds, causing StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.]

242.reason: [When encountering a single '&', an error is correctly reported, but the lexer still continues processing and may later attempt to output tokens; however, according to the problem statement, for erroneous input only the error should be output. But more critically, if pos is at the last character, pos++ will cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when accessing rawCode.charAt(pos).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', an error is correctly reported, but the lexer still continues processing and may later attempt to output tokens; however, according to the problem statement, for erroneous input only the error should be output. But more critically, if pos is at the last character, pos++ will cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when accessing rawCode.charAt(pos).]
